title: Python Remaining Operation IR Classification

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Finish GitHub #933 by making Python operation execution inventory exhaustive across generated commands, accepting true runtime primitives explicitly, and satisfying the Python completion gate without claiming non-Python completion.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Stay Python-only. Do not migrate TypeScript, Bash, or PowerShell. Do not implement semantic report behavior as portable primitive IR if it depends on live package judgment. Do not leave compatibility-runtime-handler residue unclassified.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Classify generated Python operations as portable runtime-consumed IR or accepted hand-owned runtime primitive adapters when the operation contract already owns command shape and the runtime handler is a thin _run_*_adapter.
canonical_core.escalate_when: Escalate if a command needs new behavior beyond inventory/proof classification, if runtime handlers are not thin, or if a non-Python target must change.
canonical_core.next_action: Make python_operation_execution_inventory exhaustive and update generated package proof so full Python generated CLI completion requires either runtime-consumed IR or accepted hand-owned runtime primitive classification.
completion_criteria: Every generated Python command operation id is represented in python_operation_execution_inventory.json.
Inventory statuses distinguish portable runtime-consumed IR from accepted hand-owned runtime primitive adapters.
Full Python generated CLI completion gate is satisfied only when no compatibility-runtime-handler residue remains.
Issue #933 is updated or closed with evidence that remaining behavior is explicitly classified.
Black-box Python and Docker conformance remain green.

goal: Finish #933 by making operation execution status explicit for all generated Python commands.
Promote the Python completion state only if the inventory/proof gate shows no unaccepted compatibility handler residue.
non_goals: Do not move non-Python target conformance or runtime behavior.
Do not invent portable IR for semantic report commands that still require live package judgment.
Do not change visible command behavior except generated proof/inventory reporting.

execution_run.what happened: Made the Python operation execution inventory exhaustive, promoted the Python completion gate, and tightened generated package proof so compatibility-runtime-handler residue blocks completion.
execution_run.scope touched: python_operation_execution_inventory.json, command_package_ir.json, generated package checks, focused contract/proof tests, and this execplan.
execution_run.validations run: check_contract_tooling_surfaces, check_structured_file_inventory, check_generated_command_packages, generated command package conformance, Docker proof, focused pytest, make test-workspace, make lint-workspace, generate_command_packages --check, and git diff --check.
execution_run.result for continuation: No continuation remains for #933 after issue closeout; future work resumes from the broader codegen epic only through a newly promoted plan.
execution_run.next step: Close #933 with proof, archive this plan, commit, and push.

finished_run_review.config compliance: used configured uv run agentic-workspace entrypoint and maintained the active execplan
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low; scope is limited to Python #933 and does not claim non-Python epic completion

delegation_outcome_feedback.route skipped reason: The implementation was a narrow, already-decomposed inventory/proof update with high closeout coupling and no safe disjoint write scope left after resumption.
delegation_outcome_feedback.actual friction: archive closeout required additional explicit posterity and resume fields
delegation_outcome_feedback.decomposition adjustment: future similar lanes should keep inventory/proof classification as the delegated unit before implementation starts

proof_report.proof achieved now: Exhaustive Python operation execution inventory, full Python completion gate, generated package proof, Python/TypeScript generated conformance, Docker proof, workspace tests, and lint are green.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: Ran check_contract_tooling_surfaces, check_structured_file_inventory, check_generated_command_packages, --python-conformance, --conformance --require-node, --docker --require-docker, --docker-conformance --require-docker, --python-docker-conformance --require-docker, pytest tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q, make test-workspace, and make lint-workspace.

intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: Every generated Python command operation id is inventoried; entries are either runtime-consumed or accepted-hand-owned-runtime-primitive; command_package_ir marks Python completion gate satisfied; generated package, conformance, Docker, workspace test, and lint proof passed.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: All generated Python operations are represented in python_operation_execution_inventory.json and the Python completion gate is satisfied.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: python_operation_execution_inventory.json and command_package_ir.json now own the durable completion evidence.
execution_summary.resume from: No further action in this #933 plan after archive; resume broader codegen work only from a fresh summary and newly promoted plan.

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #933 asked to migrate remaining Python generated commands from compatibility handlers to operation IR or explicit runtime status; no compatibility-runtime-handler residue remains.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: python_operation_execution_inventory.json is exhaustive, static proof rejects missing or compatibility entries, and Python/TypeScript generated conformance plus Docker proof passed.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen if a generated Python operation is added without inventory classification or if compatibility-runtime-handler status reappears.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
